items.title: Armed conflict threatens Ethiopia’s ancient monasteries
items.description: Lake Tana in Ethiopia’s Amhara region was once a tourist hotspot, known for its picturesque shorelines and monasteries dating back to the 14th century. But since 2023, an ongoing war between the Fano militias and federal government forces has scared away the tourists. Cities are subject to shootings and grenade attacks, while rural areas face drone strikes and kidnappings for ransom. Now, the future of these ancient monasteries is uncertain. This report by our correspondent Tom Canetti.

items.title: Court finds staffer's relationship with 20yo parolee 'grossly inappropriate'
items.description: An Alice Springs parole worker found in a hotel room with the 20-year-old she was responsible for supporting has avoided prison for attempting to pervert the course of justice.

items.title: 'A lot of uncertainty' as Washington says US, Iran to resume talks
items.description: Washington and Tehran have agreed to halt a new series of tit-for-tat strikes and renew talks aimed at ending the Middle East war, a US official said Sunday. The recent strikes had threatened to upend the interim deal in place between the US and Iran, but even with the talks set to resume, the path to a lasting deal remains unclear, says France 24’s correspondent in Jerusalem Noga Tarnopolsky.

items.title: Germany news: Temperatures plunge after historic heat wave
items.description: A record-breaking heat wave has moved on, with temperatures sinking rapidly amid many storms. The foreign minister, Johann Wadephul, is heading to Washington for talks with his US counterpart, with NATO issues in focus.

items.title: Moroccan court jails 29 in major drug trafficking case
items.description: A Moroccan court handed prison sentences to 29 people, including prominent politicians and sports and business figures, in an international drug trafficking and corruption scandal that has rocked the country.

items.title: Milei shuns Wall Street to raise dollars at rate lower than 7%
items.description: Luis Caputo stuck to a strategy that many investors initially saw as too risky: secure dollars through cheaper alternative channels, buy time and wait for spreads to come down. Leer más

items.title: With or without Messi? Argentina's big question against Jordan
items.description: Saturday's rotated side could still include players eager to maintain rhythm and those seeking it. Julián Álvarez is expected to start, with changes to the defence and midfield also likely. Leer más

items.title: Blues coach Daley to answer critics after Origin III
items.description: Laurie Daley says he will address criticism about his performance as Blues coach once the State of Origin series is over.

items.title: Timbuktu without water or electricity as fuel shortage shuts down power station
items.description: Timbuktu has been without water or electricity for four days, according to a report by RFI. A monthlong fuel shortage has brought the Malian city’s thermal power station to a halt - shutting down basic services.

items.title: Frozen feasts and cool pools help Rome zoo animals survive heatwave
items.description: As a severe heatwave grips Italy, Rome's Bioparco is helping its animals stay cool with frozen treats, chilled pools and climate-controlled shelters while the capital remains under a red heat alert.

items.title: Saudi Aramco helicopter crashes, all 14 passengers killed
items.description: Saudi Arabian media report that the helicopter crashed in Ras Tanura on the country's eastern coast early on Sunday. It said that all 14 Saudi nationals on board were killed, and the cause of the crash was unclear.

items.title: China, EU commerce ministers meet amid widening trade imbalance
items.description: EU Trade Commissioner Maros Sefcovic and his Chinese counterpart are meeting in Brussels as Europe prepares countermeasures to battle a flood of cheap imports. Also, oil prices climb after a weekend of renewed strikes between the US and Iran, and South Korea unveils a massive new investment in chip production.

items.title: Ukraine strikes Russian weapons plant, Moscow fuel hub
items.description: The two attacks, in Volgograd and Vladimir regions, are part of President Zelenskyy's strategy to bring the war to the Russian people and force Putin into peace negotiations.

items.title: West Asia war updates: U.S., Iran agree to halt attacks and hold talks in Doha on June 30, report says
items.description: The two sides anticipate meeting to work ‌out dispute over Strait of Hormuz; Hezbollah says reserves right to 'defend its homeland' after Israeli strikes on Lebanon

items.title: Corruption, our daily bread
items.description: An unrelenting cascade of corruption scandals enjoys political and judicial protection, while the government negotiates key appointments to put the brakes on cases at Comodoro Py. Leer más

items.title: Germany news: New heat record at 41.7 degrees Celsius
items.description: For the third consecutive day, the DWD German Weather Service has reported a record temperature. The worst of the heat is moving east and it should start cooling soon.

items.title: Pakistan strikes eastern Afghanistan as Taliban says civilians killed
items.description: Pakistan's security forces killed at least 29 militants in ground ​and air operations along the Afghanistan border, it said on Monday, while the Afghan Taliban said at least 38 civilians were killed in airstrikes. France 24's correspodent in Islamabad, Shazaib Wahlah, as the latest.

items.title: Four fatal crashes in 12 days rock SA's Limestone Coast
items.description: Police are calling on drivers to stay attentive and slow down after four people were killed on one region's roads in just 12 days.

items.title: Lauryn Hill credited with reshaping music and inspiring generations
items.description: American musician and actress Lauryn Hill has been recognised with the inaugural Living Legend Icon Award at the BET Awards by the Black Entertainment Television network in the United States.

items.title: Dollar set for best month in nearly a year as bulls grow confident
Supreme Court orders Milei government to fund universities
items.description: Ruling upholds university financing law vetoed by the President and ends a two-year legal battle over funding, salaries and student support. Leer más

items.title: Australia's Firmus Technologies strikes AI access deal with Nvidia
Here's what southern Europeans know about surviving heat waves
items.description: People in countries like Greece, Italy and Spain have long lived with high temperatures. Here's what more northerly countries can learn from them about managing heat waves as they become more common.

items.title: Messi to start dead-rubber World Cup group match on bench
items.description: "Leo will come on later and I’ve already confirmed the team, but we’re going to announce it tomorrow," coach Lionel Scaloni told reporters on Friday. Leer más

items.title: Late goal gives Canada first-ever World Cup knockout stage victory
items.description: Canada beat South Africa 1-0 to reach the World Cup last ​16 for the first time in their history.

items.title: Messi in Dallas, messy down here
items.description: The separation of powers is alive and well in Argentina when it comes to corruption – regardless of whether the issue is Cabinet Chief Manuel Adorni’s assets or the up to US$20 million stacked in the San Vicente mansion, the knee-jerk response of the political world is: “The matter belongs to the courts.” Leer más

items.title: First dinosaur bone from Antarctica found in a drawer
items.description: The fossil, collected in Antarctica in 1985, is part of the tail of a beast called a Titanosaur.

items.title: Race against the clock in Venezuela to find survivors of twin quakes
items.description: Nearly 69,000 people have been reported missing following the disaster that left at least 3,200 people injured and thousands homeless.

items.title: DGS withdraws restriction on the movement of Indian ships in the Strait of Hormuz
items.description: The advisory against deploying Indian seafarers in the conflict zones also stands withdrawn

items.title: Venezuela declares state of emergency after powerful twin earthquakes
items.description: Two massive earthquakes of magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 struck Venezuela on Wednesday evening; It remains unknown whether the incident caused fatalities, but some buildings collapsed and residents were driven into the streets in panic. Leer más

items.title: United States hopes to get share of DR Congo copper production
items.description: The ore is used in many products including electronic devices like smartphones and computers, as well as electrical wiring, motors, and generators.

items.title: U.S.-Iran peace talks in question after weekend attacks in the Gulf
items.description: Iran and the U.S. traded attacks over the weekend, throwing into question technical talks that were set to take place this week to advance the interim deal agreed earlier this month.

items.title: Nurse says emergency procedures plan at Maradona's home care site were 'not clear'
items.description: A nurse who treated Diego Maradona says emergency procedures during the football icon's home hospitalisation were poorly defined as the trial into his death continues. Leer más

items.title: Praise pours in for ‘true Canadian heroes’ after last-minute World Cup win
items.description: From the prime minister to sport celebrities and fans on social media, Canadians have revelled in their team's win.

items.title: Military-age Ukrainian men could lose EU protection
items.description: The European Commission has suggested withdrawing protection status for military-age Ukrainian males. A number of EU countries support the move — and so does Kyiv.

items.title: Bayer: Why so many Roundup weedkiller lawsuits?
items.description: Bayer was sued for failing to add cancer warnings to its controversial pesticide Roundup when US regulators opposed adding one. DW asks if the latest Supreme Court win will shield the chemical giant from paying billions.

items.title: The end of the beginning
items.description: No less than two-thirds of Messi’s record-breaking 18 World Cup goals were scored since turning 35 – in other words, at the age of 'Papi Fútbol,' he is deadlier than in the prime of youth. Leer más

items.title: US close to allowing Anthropic to restore Fable 5 model, Axios reports
Iraq: Ashoura in Karbala draws thousands of Shiite pilgrims for rituals
items.description: Thousands of Shiite pilgrims mark Ashoura in Karbala, mourning Imam Hussein’s 680 AD death with processions, prayers and reinforced security.

items.title: Russia's Putin promises security as Ukraine ramps up attacks
items.description: The Russian leader condemned what he called "terrorist attacks on our territory and infrastructure facilities." Ukraine has targeted oil refineries and weapons factories, causing massive disruption in recent weeks.

items.title: Fitness brand founder and Olympian Les Mills dies aged 91
items.description: The Les Mills International brand is now known globally for choreographed workouts set to music, like Body Pump and Body Combat.

items.title: Baidu's AI chip unit Kunlunxin targets $50 billion Hong Kong IPO, The Information reports
US and Iran exchange strikes and accuse each other of violating ceasefire
items.description: Iran says it has launched retaliatory attacks at US infrastructure in Kuwait and Bahrain after the US said it hit multiple targets across Iran.

items.title: US, Iran to halt strikes "for now," to meet in Doha over Hormuz Strait
items.description: During negotiations in Switzerland a week ago, the U.S. delegation agreed with Iran to establish a "hotline" between the U.S. military and Iran's Islamic Revolution Guard Corps to coordinate traffic in the strait.

items.title: British PM Rishi Sunak avoids wipeout in key local elections
items.description: By-elections were seen as an indicator of the two main parties' prospects at a time when voters are struggling with high inflation, strikes and rising mortgage rates.

items.title: German lawyers: Ban on far-right AfD 'likely successful'
items.description: A new report by legal experts has found that efforts to ban Germany's far-right Alternative for Germany (AfD) could be successful — but there would be political dangers to such a move.

items.title: Surgeon sounded 'exhausted' a week before fatal crash, brother says
items.description: The brother of Royal Hobart Hospital doctor Artyom Avetisyan says the cardiothoracic surgery registrar sounded "extremely exhausted" in the week before his crash death.
